title: Add parsing of verilog module port aliases (fixes #3334)
body:
Hi this is a naive attempt of adding module port aliases.
I created wires for the internal names and connected those to the port aliases.
Parsing this:
```verilog
module foo (
         .another_name_for_a(a),
	 .another_name_for_b(b),
	 .another_name_for_c(c)
);

input a;
input b;
output c;

assign c = a ^ b;

endmodule


```
gives
```
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\foo'.
Dumping AST before simplification:
    AST_MODULE <bug.v:1.1-13.10> [0x55a44b774890] str='\foo'
      AST_WIRE <bug.v:7.7-7.8> [0x55a44b774f70] str='\another_name_for_a' input port=1
      AST_WIRE <bug.v:8.7-8.8> [0x55a44b7750d0] str='\another_name_for_b' input port=2
      AST_WIRE <bug.v:9.8-9.9> [0x55a44b775210] str='\another_name_for_c' output port=3
      AST_ASSIGN <bug.v:11.8-11.17> [0x55a44b7858e0]
        AST_IDENTIFIER <bug.v:11.8-11.9> [0x55a44b774e30] str='\c'
        AST_BIT_XOR <bug.v:11.12-11.17> [0x55a44b775600]
          AST_IDENTIFIER <bug.v:11.12-11.13> [0x55a44b775380] str='\a'
          AST_IDENTIFIER <bug.v:11.16-11.17> [0x55a44b7754c0] str='\b'
      AST_WIRE <bug.v:0.0-0.0> [0x55a44b785a20] str='\a'
      AST_WIRE <bug.v:0.0-0.0> [0x55a44b785b60] str='\b'
      AST_WIRE <bug.v:0.0-0.0> [0x55a44b785ca0] str='\c'
      AST_ASSIGN <bug.v:0.0-0.0> [0x55a44b785de0]
        AST_IDENTIFIER <bug.v:0.0-0.0> [0x55a44b785f20] str='\another_name_for_c'
        AST_IDENTIFIER <bug.v:0.0-0.0> [0x55a44b786060] str='\c'
      AST_ASSIGN <bug.v:0.0-0.0> [0x55a44b7861a0]
        AST_IDENTIFIER <bug.v:0.0-0.0> [0x55a44b7862e0] str='\b'
        AST_IDENTIFIER <bug.v:0.0-0.0> [0x55a44b786420] str='\another_name_for_b'
      AST_ASSIGN <bug.v:0.0-0.0> [0x55a44b7865f0]
        AST_IDENTIFIER <bug.v:0.0-0.0> [0x55a44b786730] str='\a'

--- END OF AST DUMP ---
Dumping Verilog AST before simplification:
    module foo(another_name_for_a, another_name_for_b, another_name_for_c);
      input another_name_for_a;
      input another_name_for_b;
      output another_name_for_c;
      wire a;
      wire b;
      wire c;
      assign c = (a)^(b);
      assign another_name_for_c = c;
      assign b = another_name_for_b;
      assign a = another_name_for_a;
    endmodule
--- END OF AST DUMP ---
Dumping AST after simplification:

      AST_WIRE <bug.v:8.7-8.8> [0x55a44b7750d0] str='\another_name_for_b' input basic_prep port=2 range=[0:0]
      AST_WIRE <bug.v:9.8-9.9> [0x55a44b775210] str='\another_name_for_c' output basic_prep port=3 range=[0:0]
      AST_ASSIGN <bug.v:11.8-11.17> [0x55a44b7858e0] basic_prep
        AST_IDENTIFIER <bug.v:11.8-11.9> [0x55a44b774e30 -> 0x55a44b785ca0] str='\c' basic_prep
        AST_BIT_XOR <bug.v:11.12-11.17> [0x55a44b775600] basic_prep
          AST_IDENTIFIER <bug.v:11.12-11.13> [0x55a44b775380 -> 0x55a44b785a20] str='\a' basic_prep
          AST_IDENTIFIER <bug.v:11.16-11.17> [0x55a44b7754c0 -> 0x55a44b785b60] str='\b' basic_prep
      AST_WIRE <bug.v:0.0-0.0> [0x55a44b785a20] str='\a' basic_prep range=[0:0]
      AST_WIRE <bug.v:0.0-0.0> [0x55a44b785b60] str='\b' basic_prep range=[0:0]

      AST_ASSIGN <bug.v:0.0-0.0> [0x55a44b7861a0] basic_prep
        AST_IDENTIFIER <bug.v:0.0-0.0> [0x55a44b7862e0 -> 0x55a44b785b60] str='\b' basic_prep
        AST_IDENTIFIER <bug.v:0.0-0.0> [0x55a44b786420 -> 0x55a44b7750d0] str='\another_name_for_b' basic_prep

--- END OF AST DUMP ---
Dumping Verilog AST after simplification:

      input another_name_for_a;
      input another_name_for_b;

```
